title: Insurtech Root Inc. Results Improve but Remain at a Net Loss
text: Root Inc. reported a net loss of $147.4 million for full-year 2023, representing substantial improvement from the $298 million loss in 2022. The Columbus, Ohio-based insurtech company demonstrated progress across several indicators. Q4 2023 net loss was $24 million compared to $58.3 million in Q4 2022. Combined ratio for 2023 was 133.2, improved from 195 in 2022. Net premiums written were $573.2 million annually, up from $269 million in 2022. Co-founder and CEO Alex Timm stated that Root significantly bolstered its pricing and underwriting technology while optimizing our expense structure during 2023. He emphasized that the company achieved scale in the business and positioned itself as one of the fastest-growing auto insurers nationally. Timm indicated the company could reach profitability in the near term by reducing marketing investments, but acknowledged this approach would sacrifice growth opportunities and long-term shareholder value.
